description_long: This is a tactical fixed-blade knife featuring a robust, single-edged blade with a partially serrated spine near the handle. The blade is metallic gray, with a sharp pointed tip and a straight cutting edge, designed for both slicing and piercing tasks. The handle is black, ergonomically contoured with finger grooves for a secure grip, and appears to be made of a durable synthetic material, possibly polymer or rubberized plastic. A visible fastener or rivet secures the handle to the tang, and the handle has a lanyard hole at the rear for added utility. The knife lacks any visible decorative elements, focusing on functionality and durability. This type of knife is commonly used in urban environments for utility, survival, or tactical purposes.
description: A tactical fixed-blade knife with a metallic gray, partially serrated blade and a black, contoured ergonomic handle. Designed for utility and durability, it features a pointed tip and finger grooves for a secure grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the metallic blade with serrations and the black ergonomic handle with finger grooves.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the thinness of the blade and handle, with the pointed tip visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's profile, serrations, and the handle's contours and lanyard hole.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the slim profile of the knife and the alignment of the blade and handle.
attribute_blade_type: straight, single-edged, partially serrated, pointed tip
attribute_handle_design: contoured black polymer with finger grooves and lanyard hole
